steps.title: Understand the goal and what the notes say
steps.content: The student wants to explain why brine pools are toxic — that means the answer must name a cause and link it to the toxicity. Scanning the notes, two are relevant: “The water in brine pools is three to eight times saltier than the surrounding ocean” and “The extreme saltiness of this water makes it toxic to most sea life.” Together they form a cause-and-effect chain: extreme saltiness $\Rightarrow$ toxicity.

steps.title: Eliminate three choices, confirm one
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'The water in brine pools is toxic to most sea life because it is three to eight times saltier than the surrounding ocean.': Names the cause (saltiness) and links it to the effect (toxicity) in a single sentence. ✓
  • Choice B — 'The brine pools in the Gulf of Aqaba are toxic to most sea life and were discovered by researchers in 2022.': States the location and date of discovery but never explains why the pools are toxic. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Though brine pools are toxic to most sea life, some bacteria can survive there.': Pivots to bacteria survival — doesn't address the cause of toxicity at all. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Brine pools are salty underwater lakes that sit on the ocean floor.': Describes what brine pools are, not why they are toxic. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
